message:
Scaffold CI workflows: tests, npm publish, GH Pages

Three workflows under .github/workflows/, gated on configuration the
maintainer needs to do once before they fire:

- test.yml — runs npm test on every push/PR to main. Trivial; safe to
  enable as soon as it's merged.
- publish.yml — npm publish on `v*` tag push. Refuses to publish if the
  tag doesn't match package.json's version. Uses npm provenance. Requires
  NPM_TOKEN secret in repo settings.
- pages.yml — builds web/ and deploys to GitHub Pages on every push to
  main. Requires Pages source set to "GitHub Actions" in repo settings;
  if the site lives at github.io/capnwasm/ rather than a custom domain
  the Vite base path needs adjusting.

The follow-ups doc lists those one-time setup steps so the next person
picking this up doesn't have to dig.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+name: pages
+
+# Builds web/ on every push to main and deploys to GitHub Pages.
+# Required repo configuration:
+#   - Settings → Pages → Source: "GitHub Actions"
+#   - The Vite base path in web/vite.config.ts is "/" by default; if the site
+#     is hosted at https://<user>.github.io/capnwasm/ rather than a custom
+#     domain, set base to "/capnwasm/" before merging this workflow.
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+  pages: write
+  id-token: write
+
+# Avoid two concurrent deploys racing each other; cancel-in-progress is off
+# because we want every commit to ship its corresponding deploy if possible.
+concurrency:
+  group: "pages"
+  cancel-in-progress: false
+
+jobs:
+  build:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    defaults:
+      run:
+        working-directory: web
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Set up Node
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+          cache: npm
+          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
+
+      - name: Install root deps (the vite plugin imports from ../js)
+        working-directory: ${{ github.workspace }}
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Install web deps
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Build site
+        run: npm run build
+
+      - name: Upload Pages artifact
+        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
+        with:
+          path: web/dist
+
+  deploy:
+    needs: build
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    environment:
+      name: github-pages
+      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
+    steps:
+      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
+        id: deployment
+        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4

@@ -0,0 +1,52 @@
+name: publish
+
+# Triggered when a tag matching v* is pushed (e.g. `git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags`).
+# A manual dispatch is also allowed for re-publishing without a new tag.
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - "v*"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+# Required repo configuration before this can run:
+#   - Settings → Secrets → Actions → NPM_TOKEN  (an npm automation token with
+#     publish access to the `capnwasm` package)
+#   - The version field in package.json matches the pushed tag (without `v`).
+#     The workflow refuses to publish if they disagree.
+
+jobs:
+  publish:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: read
+      id-token: write   # for npm provenance
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Set up Node
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Run tests before publishing
+        run: npm test
+
+      - name: Verify tag matches package.json version
+        if: github.event_name == 'push'
+        run: |
+          TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}"
+          PKG=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")
+          if [ "$TAG" != "$PKG" ]; then
+            echo "Tag v$TAG does not match package.json version $PKG"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+
+      - name: Publish to npm
+        run: npm publish --provenance --access public
+        env:
+          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+name: tests
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main]
+
+jobs:
+  test:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Set up Node
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install
+        run: npm ci
+
+      # The wasm artifacts in dist/ are committed, so the test suite (which
+      # imports dist/inlined.mjs) runs without a wasm rebuild. Reproducing
+      # the wasm build in CI requires zig + binaryen and is left out here on
+      # purpose — those tools change less than the JS code, and a separate
+      # workflow can run them on a schedule when we want to verify the
+      # checked-in wasm matches a fresh build.
+      - name: Run unit tests
+        run: npm test

@@ -139,14 +139,24 @@ WASI shim further, replacing some C++ inner loops with WAT.
 Diminishing returns; below ~35 KB there's nothing major to grab without
 rewriting the wasm in hand-tuned WAT — a lot of maintenance for a couple of KB.
 
-## 9. CI / publishing automation
+## 9. CI / publishing automation — scaffolded
 
-No release workflow. `npm publish` is manual, docs site deploy is manual, the
-inspector URL is hand-coded. For a serious project this matters; for an
-internal-blog-post project it doesn't.
+**Update (2026-04-30):** three workflows landed under `.github/workflows/`.
 
-Cost: half a day to wire up GitHub Actions for `npm publish` on tag push and
-`web/dist` → GitHub Pages on main push.
+- `test.yml` — runs `npm test` on every push/PR to main.
+- `publish.yml` — `npm publish` on `v*` tag push (also manual-dispatchable).
+  Verifies the tag matches `package.json`'s version before publishing.
+  Uses npm provenance.
+- `pages.yml` — builds `web/` and deploys to GitHub Pages on every push
+  to main.
+
+Required one-time repo configuration before the workflows actually fire:
+
+- Settings → Secrets → Actions → `NPM_TOKEN` (npm automation token with
+  publish access to the `capnwasm` package).
+- Settings → Pages → Source set to "GitHub Actions".
+- If the Pages URL is `https://<user>.github.io/capnwasm/` rather than a
+  custom domain, set `base: "/capnwasm/"` in `web/vite.config.ts`.
 
 ---
